article_overview.title: Hydroxypropyl Methylcellulose as Hydrogel Matrix and Citric Acid-Locust Bean Gum as Negative Matrix for Controlled Release Tablet
article_overview.objective: To determine the optimum concentration of HPMC and CA-LBG for controlled release tablets and to study their effects on tablet properties and drug release kinetics.

abstract.purpose: To determine the optimum concentration of HPMC as hydrogel matrix and CA-LBG as negative matrix for controlled release tablet formulation and to determine the effect of CA-LBG and HPMC.
abstract.methods: Simplex lattice design for optimization; wet granulation; ketoprofen as model drug.
abstract.results: HPMC and CA-LBG increased angle of repose, tap index, hardness, friability, and ketoprofen release. Their interaction increased angle of repose, tap index, hardness but decreased friability and release. Optimum concentrations: HPMC 32.97%, CA-LBG 17.03%. Release kinetics for optimum tablets followed zero order.
abstract.conclusions: CA-LBG acts as a negative matrix causing tablet disintegration into granules; HPMC gel from granules controls release; no ghost matrix formed.

introduction.background: Controlled release tablets prolong therapeutic effect. HPMC is a common hydrogel matrix. CA-LBG is a new ester polymer with lower solubility and viscosity, synthesized from locust bean gum and citric acid using HCl catalyst and UV light.
introduction.novelty: CA-LBG as a negative matrix causes tablet disintegration into granules; HPMC matrix gel from granules controls release; avoids formation of large HPMC gel lumps (ghost matrix).

materials_origin.distilled_water: Sterilized water for injection, PT. Otsuka Indonesia
materials_origin.hydroxypropyl_methylcellulose: Methocel K4M CR Premium USP/EP, Colorcon, Singapore

experimental_design.responses: Angle of repose, tap index, hardness, friability, ketoprofen release (10 h)

CA_LBG_preparation.method: LBG (3.55e-6 mol) swelled in 50 mL warm distilled water (55-60°C), CA (21.00e-3 mol) and HCl (57.40e-3 mol) added, homogenized 10 min, irradiated with 254 nm UV for 100 min, precipitated with acetone, washed with distilled water-acetone, dried at room temperature.

characterization.FTIR: Peak at 1736.02 cm⁻¹ indicating ester carbonyl group (C=O).

NMR.1H_NMR: Paired twin peaks at δ 2.926/2.894 ppm and δ 2.746/2.714 ppm (CH₂ of CA); sharp peaks at δ 3.996-3.309 ppm (H atoms of mannose and galactose).
NMR.13C_NMR: Peaks at δ 176.905 and 173.507 ppm (C=O); δ 73.354 ppm (central C of CA); δ 43.349 ppm (CH₂ of CA); peaks at δ 100.182-60.540 ppm (C atoms of mannose and galactose).

CA_LBG_preparation.absorbance: Not applicable; FTIR used for structural confirmation.

tablet_fabrication.process: Granules prepared by mixing HPMC and lactose monohydrate (50%) in cubic mixer; moistened with CA-LBG dispersed in distilled water (±5 mL); sieved (mesh No. 18); dried at 50°C for 15 min (RH 2-5%); re-sieved (mesh No. 20); mixed with ketoprofen (100 mg) (3:1); compressed into 400 mg tablets with hardness ≥13 kp using single punch machine (Korch, Germany).
tablet_fabrication.friability_determination: Erweka drum friability tester, 4 min at 25 rpm, n=6500 mg
tablet_fabrication.consolidation_index: Tap index calculated as TI[%] = (V0 - V1)/V0 × 100% (eq 2)

equipment.drying: Oven (50°C, 15 min, RH 2-5%) and moisture analyzer (OHAUS)
equipment.dissolution: USP II paddle apparatus (Electrolab TDT-08L, India)

batch_data.factors_influencing: HPMC concentration (40%) slows release; CA-LBG (10%) causes some disintegration.
High CA-LBG (20%) accelerates disintegration and release.
High HPMC (37.5%) forms viscous gel; CA-LBG (12.5%) moderates release.
Optimum HPMC:CA-LBG ratio (32.97:17.03) forms constant thickness gel.

batch_data.weight_variation_%: Not calculated, weight shown as mean ± SD (401.34 ± 1.69 mg)

experimental_data.key_results: Optimum HPMC and CA-LBG concentrations: 32.97% and 17.03%, respectively. Optimum tablets showed zero-order release kinetics with 80% drug release at 10 h.

statistical_analysis.ANOVA: Pred R-Squared and Adj R-Squared for all responses had differences <0.2; Adeq Precision >4 for all models, indicating acceptability.
statistical_analysis.t_test_verification: No significant difference between predicted (Go) and experimental (Ga, Gb, Gc) for all parameters (T values: angle of repose 0.008, tap index 1.096, hardness 0.728, friability 1.559, release 2.657).

zero_and_first_order_release.zero_order: Optimum tablets (Ga, Gb, Gc) followed zero-order kinetics.
zero_and_first_order_release.first_order: Not observed as the primary model for any batch.
